execution_contract: Require an explicit Slack scope before reading Slack; do not broad-scan workspaces, all channels, all DMs, or unknown unread surfaces.

When no time window is provided, compute the effective window with `python3 system/scripts/chat_intake_state.py window --platform slack --scope <SCOPE>`; default to 5 business days unless the manifest has a newer successful processed timestamp for that scope.
Use only read-only Slack search/read/thread/user/canvas operations.
Never send, schedule, draft, reply, react, edit, delete, pin, bookmark, upload, create canvases/files, or mutate Slack content/workspace state.
Preserve unread state; do not use tools that mark messages read/unread, set read cursors, acknowledge notifications, or clear unread indicators.
Save searchable Slack chat transcripts under `3. Meetings/chat-transcripts/slack/` and route accepted work only to local repo files.

Scan saved Slack transcripts for referenced Jira/Confluence items, fetch only those items with read-only Atlassian connector operations, and save local artifacts under `3. Meetings/context-artifacts/atlassian/` with full `source_url` values.
Never create, edit, transition, comment, assign, add worklogs, update pages, delete, or mutate Jira or Confluence during Slack intake.
Write a local Slack run report under `3. Meetings/reports/slack-runs/` and record successful local processing in `3. Meetings/chat-transcripts/_manifest.json`.

Call out issues and recommendations for connector gaps, read-state uncertainty, missing/broad scope, Atlassian URL resolution/fetch failures, duplicate skips, reference cap skips, and task routing conflicts.
Require an explicit Teams scope before reading Teams; do not broad-scan all Teams, all channels, all chats, or unknown unread surfaces.

When no time window is provided, compute the effective window with `python3 system/scripts/chat_intake_state.py window --platform teams --scope <SCOPE>`; default to 5 business days unless the manifest has a newer successful processed timestamp for that scope.
Use only read-only Teams connector operations when surfaced; otherwise require user-provided export/text for unread-preserving intake.
Never send, draft, reply, create chats/channels, post channel messages, react, edit, delete, upload, create Planner tasks, or mutate Teams/Microsoft state.
Preserve unread state; do not use tools that mark messages read/unread, set read cursors, acknowledge notifications, or clear unread indicators.
Save searchable Teams chat transcripts under `3. Meetings/chat-transcripts/teams/` and route accepted work only to local repo files.

Scan saved Teams transcripts for referenced Jira/Confluence items, fetch only those items with read-only Atlassian connector operations, and save local artifacts under `3. Meetings/context-artifacts/atlassian/` with full `source_url` values.
Never create, edit, transition, comment, assign, add worklogs, update pages, delete, or mutate Jira or Confluence during Teams intake.
Write a local Teams run report under `3. Meetings/reports/teams-runs/` and record successful local processing in `3. Meetings/chat-transcripts/_manifest.json`.

Call out issues and recommendations for connector gaps, read-state uncertainty, missing/broad scope, Atlassian URL resolution/fetch failures, duplicate skips, reference cap skips, and task routing conflicts.
Run scoped Slack and Teams intake independently, using each platform workflow's read-only and unread-preservation rules.
Require explicit platform scopes and use `chat_intake_state.py window` for any platform scope without a user-provided time window.
Never send, draft, schedule, reply, react, edit, delete, create chats/channels/canvases/files/Planner tasks, or mutate source-system state.
Save searchable platform transcripts under `3. Meetings/chat-transcripts/slack/` and `3. Meetings/chat-transcripts/teams/`.

Scan saved platform transcripts for referenced Jira/Confluence items, fetch only those references with read-only Atlassian connector operations, and save local artifacts under `3. Meetings/context-artifacts/atlassian/` with full `source_url` values.
Never create, edit, transition, comment, assign, add worklogs, update pages, delete, or mutate Jira or Confluence during communication intake.
Merge and deduplicate candidate tasks after evidence collection; route accepted work only to local repo files.
Write a combined communication run report under `3. Meetings/reports/chat-runs/`.

Call out issues and recommendations for connector gaps, read-state uncertainty, missing/broad scope, Atlassian URL resolution/fetch failures, duplicate skips, reference cap skips, and task routing conflicts.
Run `python3 system/scripts/transcript_pipeline.py prepare --business-days 10 --json` before synthesis.
Process only packet files listed in the prepare output; do not broad-scan transcripts for work selection.
Use hybrid appendix summaries: source path, transcript SHA256, run id, key evidence, and Routed Updates; do not append full raw transcript by default.

When including Slack, use only read-only Slack search/read/thread/user/canvas operations; never send, schedule, draft, or mutate Slack state/read cursors, and preserve unread state until the user changes it manually.
Run `python3 system/scripts/transcript_pipeline.py validate --run-id <RUN_ID> --json` after summaries and durable updates are written.
Run `python3 system/scripts/transcript_pipeline.py recent --limit 5 --md` for the final compact meeting output.

vacuum.note: Guardrail: keep cleanup bounded to the repo's PM memory lanes. Do not archive active code or configurations, and confirm if the scope is ambiguous.

update.note: Guardrail: inspect current branch and worktree before running. If the repo is dirty or not on main, summarize the risk before executing the update flow.
